# datadog_incident_user_defined_role (Resource)

Provides a Datadog incident user-defined role resource. This can be used to create and manage custom incident roles that responders can be assigned to, scoped to an incident type.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "datadog_incident_type" "security" {
  name = "Security Incident"
}

# A custom role scoped to the Security incident type
resource "datadog_incident_user_defined_role" "security_lead" {
  name             = "Security Lead"
  description      = "Owns the security response for an incident"
  incident_type_id = datadog_incident_type.security.id

  policy {
    is_single = true
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Name of the user-defined role. Must be between 1 and 255 characters and cannot be a reserved name ("Incident Commander" or "Responder").
- `incident_type_id` (String) The ID of the incident type this role is scoped to. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

### Optional

- `description` (String) Description of the user-defined role. Can have a maximum of 1024 characters.
- `policy` (Attributes) The policy governing how the role can be assigned. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--policy))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of the user-defined role.
- `created` (String) Timestamp when the user-defined role was created.
- `modified` (String) Timestamp when the user-defined role was last modified.
- `created_by_user_id` (String) The ID of the user who created the role.
- `last_modified_by_user_id` (String) The ID of the user who last modified the role.

<a id="nestedatt--policy"></a>
### Nested Schema for `policy`

Optional:

- `is_single` (Boolean) Whether at most one responder can hold this role at a time on a given incident. Defaults to `false`.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
terraform import datadog_incident_user_defined_role.example "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"
```
